title: linum pallasianum subsp. pallasianum
intro: Linum pallasianum subsp. pallasianum, also known as Pallas's flax or Pallas' flax, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Linaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Linum pallasianum subsp. pallasianum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can be utilized through herbal preparations such as tea infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts.
